labels.analysis: The MCP Server "io.github.hellosg/coderegistry" provides 16 tools focused on development tools tasks. Core capabilities include creation and generation, data access and reading, deletion and cleanup, modification and updates. Key tools: create_account, list_projects, get_project, create_project, delete_project.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Development Tools" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: git, code, docker, endpoint, registry.

tools.name: create_account
tools.description: Creates a new Code Registry account and returns API credentials. Cold starts can cause the first request to time out; retry with backoff. No API key required.

integrator_id.description: Optional integrator identifier (used for API key scoping).

inputSchema.description: Creates a new account and returns an API key.

tools.name: list_projects
tools.description: Lists all projects for the authenticated team. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get_project
tools.description: Returns a specific project by id. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: create_project
tools.description: Creates a project for a team user. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: delete_project
tools.description: Deletes a project and its vaults. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: list_vaults
tools.description: Lists vaults within a project. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get_vault
tools.description: Returns a specific vault by id. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: create-code-vault
tools.description: Creates a code vault and queues the initial analysis. IMPORTANT: Always use LOCAL_AGENT (or AGENT) unless the user explicitly requests remote analysis or Docker is unavailable. LOCAL_AGENT runs locally via Docker; source code stays on the machine and only anonymous analysis data is uploaded. A Git repo is only required if you want Git-based insights (git-history, code-contributors). If Git insights are needed, the mounted path must be a standard Git repo containing a .git directory (not a .git file pointing to a worktree). The response includes next_steps.commands that must be executed to start the local agent. Use GIT/FILE_ARCHIVE only when the user provides a reachable URL plus read credentials (username/password or token). Cold starts can cause the first request to time out; retry with backoff. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

inputSchema.description: Creates a code vault and queues the initial analysis.

source_type.description: Code source type. ALWAYS use LOCAL_AGENT (or AGENT) unless the user explicitly requests remote analysis or Docker is unavailable. LOCAL_AGENT runs locally via Docker; source code stays on the machine and only anonymous analysis data is uploaded. A Git repo is only required for Git-based insights (git-history, code-contributors). If you need those, ensure the mounted path is a standard Git repo containing a .git directory (not a .git file pointing to a worktree). Use GIT/FILE_ARCHIVE only when the user provides a reachable URL plus read credentials (username/password or token). Do NOT attempt GIT without credentials.

source_url.description: Repository or archive URL (required for GIT/FILE_ARCHIVE; ignored for LOCAL_AGENT/AGENT). Must be reachable by the platform. FILE_ARCHIVE accepts .zip/.tar URLs and supported Google Drive/Dropbox links.

username.description: Source username (required for private GIT repos; must have read access).

password.description: Source password or personal access token (required for private GIT repos; must have read access).

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: reanalyze-code-vault
tools.description: Creates a new analysis version for an existing code vault using its existing source settings. For LOCAL_AGENT, the response includes next_steps.commands and the local agent must be run again. For GIT/FILE_ARCHIVE, the re-analysis of the original code source is queued automatically. Note: summary/results/report tools always return the latest version only, so reanalysis replaces access to prior version data. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

user_id.description: Optional user id for attribution (from create_account or get_account).

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: delete-code-vault
tools.description: Deletes a code vault and related analysis data. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get-code-vault-summary
tools.description: Returns the latest version/status info for a vault. Analysis is async; if status is 'processing', poll with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, 40s, max 60s). Analysis can be as quick as 20-30 minutes for under 500,000 lines of code. Larger codebases can take much longer, especially with the security scan. This endpoint always returns the latest version only; once reanalysis starts, prior versions are no longer accessible here. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get-code-vault-results
tools.description: Returns full facet results and AI insights for a vault. Analysis is async; if status is 'processing', poll with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, 40s, max 60s). Analysis can be as quick as 20-30 minutes for under 500,000 lines of code. Larger codebases can take much longer, especially with the security scan. Facet meanings are documented in resources://docs/facets; AI Quotient is a code-quality metric (not AI-generated code). AI insights can take a few minutes after analysis completes; if ai_insights is empty, poll again and check ai_insights_status per facet (ready/processing/not_available). This endpoint always returns the latest version only; once reanalysis starts, prior versions are no longer accessible here. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get-code-vault-reports
tools.description: Returns report URLs (snapshot/comparison) for a vault. Completion rules: if version is 1.0.0, snapshot indicates completion and comparison is null; for versions above 1.0.0, comparison indicates completion. If not ready, retry with exponential backoff (5s, 10s, 20s, 40s, max 60s). This endpoint always returns the latest version only; once reanalysis starts, prior versions are no longer accessible here. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: get_account
tools.description: Returns the team owner account information. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: delete_account
tools.description: Deletes the team account plus all projects and vaults. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.

tools.name: rotate_api_key
tools.description: Issues a fresh integrator API key. Requires X-API-Key (existing users can generate an API key in the web app). If headers aren't supported, pass api_key in arguments.

integrator_id.description: Optional integrator identifier (defaults to existing integrator or 'default').

api_key.description: Optional API key for clients that cannot set X-API-Key headers.
